                                              ) 
                                           
                                           } 
                                        
                                      
                                     for some distinct  
                                     
                                        u 
                                     
                                     ,  
                                     
                                        v 
                                      
                                     in  
                                     
                                        
                                           [ 
                                           n 
                                           ] 
                                        
                                        ∖ 
                                        
                                           { 
                                           i 
                                           , 
                                           j 
                                           , 
                                           k 
                                           , 
                                           l 
                                           } 
                                        
                                     
                                     .

Proof 
                         The specific inequalities in the two cases are valid in view of  Theorem 11 . To prove that they define facets, it suffices to exhibit respectively  
                               5 
                               ⋅ 
                               4 
                               = 
                               20 
                             and  
                               6 
                               ⋅ 
                               5 
                               = 
                               30 
                             semiorders with affinely independent characteristic vectors satisfying  (44)  with equality: in view of  Theorem 9 , it suffices to work with  
                               
                                  [ 
                                  n 
                                  ] 
                               
                             equal to  
                               
                                  { 
                                  i 
                                  , 
                                  j 
                                  , 
                                  k 
                                  , 
                                  l 
                                  , 
                                  t 
                                  } 
                               
                             or  
                               
                                  { 
                                  i 
                                  , 
                                  j 
                                  , 
                                  k 
                                  , 
                                  l 
                                  , 
                                  u 
                                  , 
                                  v 
                                  } 
                               
                            , respectively. 
                         For both inequalities, we take the same list as in the proof of  Theorem 12 , but with one small modification. It is no longer true that for every arc  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  x 
                                  , 
                                  y 
                                  ) 
                               
                             of  Type 5 , it is possible to find an arc  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  k 
                                  , 
                                  l 
                                  ) 
                               
                             such that  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  x 
                                  , 
                                  l 
                                  ) 
                               
                               ∉ 
                               B 
                             or  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  k 
                                  , 
                                  y 
                                  ) 
                               
                               ∉ 
                               B 
                            . For both inequalities, the only  Type 5  arc not satisfying this property is  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  i 
                                  , 
                                  j 
                                  ) 
                               
                            . Hence for  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  i 
                                  , 
                                  j 
                                  ) 
                               
                            , the construction given in  Type 5  does not work and we must construct another semiorder associated to the arc  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  i 
                                  , 
                                  j 
                                  ) 
                               
                             to complete the list. For the first inequality, we take  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        
                                           S 
                                        
                                        
                                           i 
                                           j 
                                        
                                     
                                     = 
                                     
                                        { 
                                        
                                           ( 
                                           i 
                                           , 
                                           j 
                                           ) 
                                        
                                        , 
                                        
                                           ( 
                                           i 
                                           , 
                                           k 
                                           ) 
                                        
                                        , 
                                        
                                           ( 
                                           i 
                                           , 
                                           l 
                                           ) 
                                        
                                        , 
                                        
                                           ( 
                                           j 
                                           , 
                                           k 
                                           ) 
                                        
                                        , 
                                        
                                           ( 
                                           j 
                                           , 
                                           l 
                                           ) 
                                        
                                        , 
                                        
                                           ( 
                                           k 
                                           , 
                                           l 
                                           ) 
                                        
                                        , 
                                        
                                           ( 
                                           t 
                                           , 
                                           k 
                                           ) 
                                        
                                        , 
                                        
                                           ( 
                                           t 
                                           , 
                                           l 
                                           ) 
                                        
                                        } 
                                     
                                  
                               
                             and for the second one we take  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        
                                           S 
                                        
                                        
                                           i 
                                           j 
                                        
                                     
                                     = 
                                     
                                        {

                                           i

                                           j

                                        ,

                                        ,

                                        ,

                                           j

                                           l

                                           v 
                                           , 
                                           k

                                        ,

                                           u

                                           k 
                                           ) 
                                        
                                        ,

                                        }

                             It is straightforward to check that  
                               
                                  
                                     S 
                                  
                                  
                                     i 
                                     j 
                                  
                               
                             is a semiorder and that  
                               
                                  
                                     χ 
                                  
                                  
                                     
                                        
                                           S 
                                        
                                        
                                           i 
                                           j 
                                        
                                     
                                  
                               
                             satisfies the inequality with equality. Putting then  
                               
                                  
                                     S 
                                  
                                  
                                     i 
                                     j 
                                  
                               
                             at the end of the list, we get a set of semiorders with affinely equivalent characteristic vectors with respectively 20 and 30 elements. 
                         We now show that if  
                               A 
                             and  
                               B 
                             satisfy the assumptions, then they are of one of the two latter forms. Because of  
                               
                                  | 
                                  A 
                                  | 
                               
                               ⩾ 
                               3 
                             and  Condition C1  in  Theorem 11 , there must exist  
                               k 
                             and  
                               l 
                             in  
                               
                                  [ 
                                  n

                               ∖ 
                               
                                  {

                               ∈

                         First, let us infer the existence of some element  
                               s 
                             in  
                               
                                  [ 
                                  n 
                                  ] 
                               
                               ∖ 
                               
                                  {

                                  )

                            . If no such element

                             exists in  
                               A 
                             we derive, from the present Assumption (II) together with our  
                               A 
                            -minimality assumption (applied to the pair  
                               
                                  (

                               B

                             in  Condition C5 : as shown in  Fig. 10

                               w

                            . This shows that the deletion of

                               w 
                            ,

                               s 
                             in  
                               
                                  [ 
                                  n 
                                  ] 
                               
                               ∖ 
                               
                                  { 
                                  j 
                                  ,

                                  } 
                               
                             the pair  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  l

                                  )

                               A

                               B 
                               ∖ 
                               
                                  { 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     i

                                     l 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  } 
                               
                             is  
                               A 
                            -minimal), there is some  
                               r 
                             in  
                               
                                  [ 
                                  n 
                                  ]

                                  { 
                                  i

                                  , 
                                  l 
                                  }

                               r 
                               ≠

                         Suppose first that both equalities  
                               r 
                               = 
                               j

                                  i

                               B 
                             by Assumption (III) and we will prove that replacing  
                               B

                               ∖ 
                               
                                  {

                                     , 
                                     y

                                  }

                               A

                             to deduce that we are in one of the two cases of the statement of the lemma we are proving. Actually, these two cases correspond to Requirements (v5) and (v6) in  Condition C5 . Hence, we only have to show that if Requirements (v1)–(v4) are satisfied we have a contradiction with the  
                               A 
                            -minimality of  
                               B 
                            . Suppose Requirement (v1) or Requirement (v3) is satisfied. Then  
                               B 
                             is not  
                               A 
                            -minimal, because replacing  
                               B

                               ∖

                                     ( 
                                     i

                                  k

                                  j 
                                  ) 
                               
                             of  
                               B

                               B 
                            . This concludes the proof when

                               A

                               B

The specific inequalities in the two cases are valid in view of  Theorem 11 . To prove that they define facets, it suffices to exhibit respectively  
                               5 
                               ⋅ 
                               4 
                               = 
                               20 
                             and  
                               6 
                               ⋅ 
                               5 
                               = 
                               30 
                             semiorders with affinely independent characteristic vectors satisfying  (44)  with equality: in view of  Theorem 9 , it suffices to work with  
                               
                                  [ 
                                  n 
                                  ] 
                               
                             equal to

                                  i

                                  l 
                                  , 
                                  u 
                                  , 
                                  v 
                                  } 
                               
                            , respectively.

For both inequalities, we take the same list as in the proof of  Theorem 12 , but with one small modification. It is no longer true that for every arc  
                               
                                  (

                                  ,

We now show that if  
                               A 
                             and  
                               B 
                             satisfy the assumptions, then they are of one of the two latter forms. Because of  
                               
                                  | 
                                  A 
                                  | 
                               
                               ⩾ 
                               3 
                             and  Condition C1  in  Theorem 11 , there must exist  
                               k 
                             and  
                               l 
                             in  
                               
                                  [ 
                                  n 
                                  ] 
                               
                               ∖ 
                               
                                  { 
                                  i 
                                  ,

                                  } 
                               
                             such that  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  k 
                                  , 
                                  l 
                                  ) 
                               
                               ∈ 
                               A 
                            .

First, let us infer the existence of some element  
                               s 
                             in  
                               
                                  [ 
                                  n 
                                  ] 
                               
                               ∖ 
                               
                                  { 
                                  j 
                                  , 
                                  k 
                                  ,

                                  }

                                  l 
                                  , 
                                  s 
                                  ) 
                               
                               ∈ 
                               A 
                            . If no such element  
                               s 
                             exists in  
                               A 
                             we derive, from the present Assumption (II) together with our  
                               A 
                            -minimality assumption (applied to the pair  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  k 
                                  , 
                                  j 
                                  ) 
                               
                             in  
                               B 
                            ), that  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  k 
                                  , 
                                  j 
                                  ) 
                               
                             cannot be anything else than a pair as  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  u 
                                  , 
                                  v 
                                  ) 
                               
                             in  Condition C5 : as shown in  Fig. 10 
                            , there exist distinct elements  
                               w 
                            ,  
                               x 
                            ,  
                               y 
                            ,  
                               z 
                             in  
                               
                                  [ 
                                  n 
                                  ] 
                               
                               ∖ 
                               
                                  { 
                                  k 
                                  , 
                                  j

                             such that the pairs  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  w 
                                  , 
                                  x 
                                  )

                                  y

                               A 
                             and  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  w

                                  k 
                                  )

                                  ( 
                                  j

                                  )

                               B 
                            . Moreover, we must have

                                  l 
                                  )

                             disjoint. Then by  Condition C1 ,  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  k

                                  y 
                                  ) 
                               
                               ∈ 
                               B 
                            . This shows that the deletion of

                               w 
                            ,  
                               x 
                            ,  
                               y 
                             and  
                               z 
                            ), a contradiction with our present assumption in the statement. We conclude that for some element  
                               s 
                             in  
                               
                                  [ 
                                  n 
                                  ] 
                               
                               ∖ 
                               
                                  { 
                                  j 
                                  , 
                                  k 
                                  ,

                                  } 
                               
                             the pair  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  l

                                  s 
                                  ) 
                               
                             is in  
                               A 
                            . Similarly (this time because  
                               B 
                               ∖ 
                               
                                  { 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     i 
                                     , 
                                     l 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  } 
                               
                             is  
                               A 
                            -minimal), there is some  
                               r 
                             in  
                               
                                  [ 
                                  n 
                                  ] 
                               
                               ∖ 
                               
                                  { 
                                  i 
                                  , 
                                  k 
                                  , 
                                  l 
                                  }

                               A 
                             (see  Fig. 11

                                  i

                                  j

                               r 
                               ≠ 
                               i 
                             and  
                               s

                               j

                               r 
                             might be equal to  
                               j 
                             and  
                               s 
                             might be equal to  
                               i

Suppose first that both equalities  
                               r 
                               = 
                               j 
                             and  
                               s 
                               = 
                               i 
                             hold (as in  Fig. 12 
                            ). We prove that the lemma holds by first establishing  
                               A 
                               = 
                               
                                  { 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     j 
                                     , 
                                     k 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  , 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     k 
                                     , 
                                     l

                                  ,

                                     l 
                                     , 
                                     i 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  } 
                               
                            . Suppose that  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  x 
                                  , 
                                  y 
                                  ) 
                               
                             is another pair of  
                               A 
                            . Then  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  i 
                                  , 
                                  y 
                                  ) 
                               
                               ∈ 
                               B 
                             by Assumption (III) and we will prove that replacing  
                               B 
                             with  
                               B 
                               ∖ 
                               
                                  { 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     i 
                                     , 
                                     y 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  } 
                               
                             leaves a valid inequality, a contradiction with the  
                               A 
                            -minimality of  
                               B 
                            . If replacing  
                               B 
                             with  
                               B 
                               ∖ 
                               
                                  { 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     i 
                                     , 
                                     y 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  } 
                               
                             gives a nonvalid inequality, then either  Condition C4  is not satisfied or  Condition C5  is not satisfied by  
                               A 
                             and  
                               B 
                               ∖ 
                               
                                  { 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     i 
                                     , 
                                     y 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  } 
                               
                            .

If  Condition C4  is not, then  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  w 
                                  , 
                                  x 
                                  ) 
                               
                               ∈ 
                               A 
                             for some  
                               w

                               ,

                               ,

                               , 
                               
                               i 
                            . But this is impossible since then

                                  w 
                                  , 
                                  l 
                                  ) 
                               
                             and  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  l 
                                  , 
                                  y 
                                  ) 
                               
                             are in  
                               B 
                             by  Condition C2 , and hence  Condition C4  is satisfied by  
                               A 
                             and  
                               B 
                               ∖ 
                               
                                  { 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     i 
                                     , 
                                     y 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  } 
                               
                            .

So if  
                               B 
                               ∖ 
                               
                                  { 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     i 
                                     , 
                                     y 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  } 
                               
                             gives a nonvalid inequality, then  Condition C5  is not satisfied. But then the three consecutive pairs of this condition must be all disjoint from  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  j 
                                  , 
                                  k 
                                  ) 
                               
                            ,  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  k 
                                  , 
                                  l 
                                  ) 
                               
                             and  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  l 
                                  , 
                                  i 
                                  ) 
                               
                            , a contradiction by  Lemma 3 . This proves  
                               A 
                               = 
                               
                                  { 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     j 
                                     , 
                                     k 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  , 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     k 
                                     , 
                                     l 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  , 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     l 
                                     , 
                                     i 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  } 
                               
                            .

Next, remember  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  i 
                                  , 
                                  l 
                                  ) 
                               
                            ,  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  k 
                                  , 
                                  j 
                                  ) 
                               
                               ∈ 
                               B 
                            . Moreover, by  Condition C2 ,  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  j 
                                  , 
                                  i 
                                  ) 
                               
                            ,  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  l 
                                  , 
                                  k 
                                  ) 
                               
                               ∈ 
                               B 
                            . We now apply  Condition C5  to the pairs  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  j 
                                  , 
                                  k 
                                  ) 
                               
                               , 
                               
                               
                                  ( 
                                  k 
                                  , 
                                  l 
                                  ) 
                               
                               , 
                               
                               
                                  ( 
                                  l 
                                  , 
                                  i 
                                  ) 
                               
                             to deduce that we are in one of the two cases of the statement of the lemma we are proving. Actually, these two cases correspond to Requirements (v5) and (v6) in  Condition C5 . Hence, we only have to show that if Requirements (v1)–(v4) are satisfied we have a contradiction with the  
                               A 
                            -minimality of  
                               B 
                            . Suppose Requirement (v1) or Requirement (v3) is satisfied. Then  
                               B 
                             is not  
                               A 
                            -minimal, because replacing  
                               B 
                             with  
                               B 
                               ∖ 
                               
                                  { 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     i 
                                     , 
                                     l 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  } 
                               
                             still preserves  Conditions C1 ,  C2 ,  C4 ,  C5 . The argument is similar for Requirements (v2) and (v4), with the pair  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  k 
                                  , 
                                  j 
                                  ) 
                               
                             of  
                               B 
                            . By the  
                               A 
                            -minimality of  
                               B 
                            , there is no further pair in  
                               B 
                            . This concludes the proof when  
                               r 
                               = 
                               j 
                             and  
                               s 
                               = 
                               i 
                            .

If  
                               r 
                               ≠ 
                               j 
                             or  
                               s 
                               ≠ 
                               i 
                            , the situation must be as in one of the cases of  Fig. 13 
                            . We deduce a contradiction between Assumption (III) and the  
                               A 
                            -minimality of  
                               B 
                             in each case. By Assumption (III), the pair  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  i 
                                  , 
                                  l 
                                  ) 
                               
                             belongs to  
                               B 
                            ; when  
                               r 
                               ≠ 
                               j 
                            , the same assumption (after replacement of  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  k 
                                  , 
                                  l 
                                  ) 
                               
                             with  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  r 
                                  , 
                                  k 
                                  ) 
                               
                            ) gives  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  r 
                                  , 
                                  j 
                                  ) 
                               
                               ∈ 
                               B 
                            .

Consider first Case 2 of  Fig. 13 . We know that  
                               B 
                               ∖ 
                               
                                  { 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     r 
                                     , 
                                     j 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  } 
                               
                             is not valid. Either  Condition C4  or  Condition C5  is not satisfied by  
                               B 
                               ∖ 
                               
                                  { 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     r 
                                     , 
                                     j 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  } 
                               
                            . By  Lemma 3 ,  
                               A 
                             does not contain two consecutive pairs  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  u 
                                  , 
                                  v 
                                  ) 
                               
                               , 
                               
                                  ( 
                                  v 
                                  , 
                                  w 
                                  )

                                  ( 
                                  r 
                                  , 
                                  k 
                                  ) 
                               
                             and  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  k 
                                  , 
                                  l 
                                  ) 
                               
                             (this would imply that  
                               B 
                               ∖ 
                               
                                  { 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     r 
                                     , 
                                     j 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  } 
                               
                             still satisfies  Conditions C1 ,  C2 ,  C4 ,  C5 , a contradiction). Hence  
                               A 
                             does not contain three consecutive pairs  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  u 
                                  , 
                                  v 
                                  )

                                  ( 
                                  v

                                  w

                                  w

                             with  
                               u

                               v

                               w

                               B 
                               ∖ 
                               
                                  { 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     r 
                                     , 
                                     j 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  } 
                               
                            . This implies then that

                                  l

                               ∖ 
                               
                                  {

                                     i 
                                     , 
                                     l

                                  } 
                               
                             is still valid. This gives the desired contradiction.

Cases 1 and 4 are similar, so we only treat Case 4. We claim that  
                               B 
                               ∖

                               A 
                             or of

                            ). □

Condition C1  implies that for a valid inequality  
                         
                            (47) 
                            
                               
                                  
                                     ∑ 
                                  
                                  
                                     a 
                                     ∈ 
                                     A 
                                  
                               
                               
                                  
                                     x 
                                  
                                  
                                     a 
                                  
                               
                               − 
                               
                                  
                                     ∑ 
                                  
                                  
                                     b 
                                     ∈ 
                                     B 
                                  
                               
                               
                                  
                                     x 
                                  
                                  
                                     b 
                                  
                               
                               ⩽ 
                               1 
                            
                         
                       the graph  
                         
                            ( 
                            
                               [ 
                               n 
                               ] 
                            
                            , 
                            A 
                            ) 
                         
                       is a PC-graph, that is, its components are isolated vertices, directed paths or directed cycles. For many of the structural forms of the PC-graph  
                         
                            ( 
                            
                               [ 
                               n 
                               ] 
                            
                            , 
                            A 
                            ) 
                         
                      , there is only one set  
                         B 
                       such that Eq.  (47)  provides a facet-defining inequality. We say that a pair  
                         
                            ( 
                            i 
                            , 
                            l 
                            ) 
                         
                       in  
                         
                            [ 
                            n 
                            ] 
                         
                       is  
                         A 
                      [ C2 ]- forced  if there exist elements  
                         j 
                       and  
                         k 
                       such that  
                         i 
                      ,  
                         j 
                      ,  
                         k 
                       and  
                         l 
                       are distinct and moreover  
                         
                            ( 
                            i 
                            , 
                            j 
                            ) 
                         
                       and  
                         
                            ( 
                            k 
                            , 
                            l 
                            ) 
                         
                       are in  
                         A 
                      . Thus the  
                         A 
                      [ C2 ]-forced pairs  
                         
                            ( 
                            i 
                            , 
                            l 
                            ) 
                         
                       are exactly those that  Condition C2  forces to be in  
                         B 
                      .  
                         Theorem 13 
                         
                            Consider for disjoint, nonempty subsets  
                            
                               A 
                             
                            and  
                            
                               B 
                             
                            of  
                            
                               
                                  
                                     A 
                                  
                                  
                                     n 
                                  
                               
                            
                            , the inequality 
                            
                               
                                  (48) 
                                  
                                     
                                        
                                           ∑ 
                                        
                                        
                                           a 
                                           ∈ 
                                           A 
                                        
                                     
                                     
                                        
                                           x 
                                        
                                        
                                           a 
                                        
                                     
                                     − 
                                     
                                        
                                           ∑ 
                                        
                                        
                                           b 
                                           ∈ 
                                           B 
                                        
                                     
                                     
                                        
                                           x 
                                        
                                        
                                           b 
                                        
                                     
                                     ⩽ 
                                     1 
                                     , 
                                  
                               
                            
                            and suppose that the graph  
                            
                               
                                  ( 
                                  
                                     [ 
                                     n 
                                     ] 
                                  
                                  , 
                                  A 
                                  ) 
                               
                             
                            is a PC-graph whose components meet at least one of the following six assumptions: 
                            
                               
                                  1. 
                                  
                                     at least one component contains two opposite pairs (of elements) in  
                                     
                                        A 
                                      
                                     (and thus no other pair); 
                                  
                               
                               
                                  2. 
                                  
                                     at least two components contain at least two pairs in  
                                     
                                        A 
                                      
                                     ; 
                                  
                               
                               
                                  3. 
                                  
                                     at least one component contains at least six pairs in  
                                     
                                        A 
                                      
                                     ; 
                                  
                               
                               
                                  4. 
                                  
                                     all nontrivial components contain exactly one pair of  
                                     
                                        A 
                                     
                                     . 
                                  
                               
                            
                            Then Eq.     (48)     describes a facet-defining inequality for  
                            
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        P 
                                     
                                  
                                  
                                     
                                        SO 
                                     
                                  
                                  
                                     n 
                                  
                               
                             
                            if and only if  
                            
                               B 
                             
                            consists exactly of the  
                            
                               A 
                             
                            [     C2 
                            ]-forced pairs.

Consider for disjoint, nonempty subsets  
                            
                               A 
                             
                            and  
                            
                               B 
                             
                            of  
                            
                               
                                  
                                     A 
                                  
                                  
                                     n 
                                  
                               
                            
                            , the inequality 
                            
                               
                                  (48) 
                                  
                                     
                                        
                                           ∑ 
                                        
                                        
                                           a 
                                           ∈ 
                                           A 
                                        
                                     
                                     
                                        
                                           x 
                                        
                                        
                                           a 
                                        
                                     
                                     − 
                                     
                                        
                                           ∑ 
                                        
                                        
                                           b 
                                           ∈ 
                                           B 
                                        
                                     
                                     
                                        
                                           x 
                                        
                                        
                                           b 
                                        
                                     
                                     ⩽ 
                                     1 
                                     , 
                                  
                               
                            
                            and suppose that the graph  
                            
                               
                                  ( 
                                  
                                     [ 
                                     n 
                                     ] 
                                  
                                  , 
                                  A 
                                  ) 
                               
                             
                            is a PC-graph whose components meet at least one of the following six assumptions: 
                            
                               
                                  1. 
                                  
                                     at least one component contains two opposite pairs (of elements) in  
                                     
                                        A 
                                      
                                     (and thus no other pair); 
                                  
                               
                               
                                  2. 
                                  
                                     at least two components contain at least two pairs in  
                                     
                                        A 
                                      
                                     ; 
                                  
                               
                               
                                  3. 
                                  
                                     at least one component contains at least six pairs in  
                                     
                                        A 
                                      
                                     ; 
                                  
                               
                               
                                  4. 
                                  
                                     all nontrivial components contain exactly one pair of  
                                     
                                        A 
                                     
                                     . 
                                  
                               
                            
                            Then Eq.     (48)     describes a facet-defining inequality for  
                            
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        P 
                                     
                                  
                                  
                                     
                                        SO 
                                     
                                  
                                  
                                     n 
                                  
                               
                             
                            if and only if  
                            
                               B 
                             
                            consists exactly of the  
                            
                               A 
                             
                            [     C2 
                            ]-forced pairs.

at least one component contains two opposite pairs (of elements) in  
                                     
                                        A 
                                      
                                     (and thus no other pair);

at least two components contain at least two pairs in  
                                     
                                        A 
                                      
                                     ;

at least one component contains at least six pairs in  
                                     
                                        A 
                                      
                                     ;

all nontrivial components contain exactly one pair of  
                                     
                                        A 
                                     
                                     .

Proof 
                         First, remember that  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  
                                     [ 
                                     n 
                                     ] 
                                  
                                  , 
                                  A 
                                  ) 
                               
                             is a PC-graph exactly if  Condition C1  holds. Next, we note that if the PC-graph  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  
                                     [ 
                                     n 
                                     ] 
                                  
                                  , 
                                  A 
                                  ) 
                               
                             satisfies one of the assumptions 1–4, then the validity of  Condition C2  implies the validity of  Conditions C4 and C5  (this is easily established). Then in the present situation  Theorem 12  entails that Eq.  (48)  gives a facet-defining inequality if and only if  
                               B 
                             contains the  
                               A 
                            [ C2 ]-forced pairs but no other pair. □

First, remember that  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  
                                     [ 
                                     n 
                                     ] 
                                  
                                  , 
                                  A 
                                  ) 
                               
                             is a PC-graph exactly if  Condition C1  holds. Next, we note that if the PC-graph  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  
                                     [ 
                                     n 
                                     ] 
                                  
                                  , 
                                  A 
                                  ) 
                               
                             satisfies one of the assumptions 1–4, then the validity of  Condition C2  implies the validity of  Conditions C4 and C5  (this is easily established). Then in the present situation  Theorem 12  entails that Eq.  (48)  gives a facet-defining inequality if and only if  
                               B 
                             contains the  
                               A 
                            [ C2 ]-forced pairs but no other pair. □

To obtain a full list of all the primary facet-defining inequalities of  
                         
                            
                               
                                  P 
                               
                            
                            
                               
                                  SO 
                               
                            
                            
                               n 
                            
                         
                      , it remains to investigate the pairs  
                         A 
                       and  
                         B 
                       as in  Theorem 12  for which the PC-graph  
                         
                            ( 
                            
                               [ 
                               n 
                               ] 
                            
                            , 
                            A 
                            ) 
                         
                       does not satisfy any of the four assumptions of  Theorem 13 . Such a PC-graph has exactly one nontrivial component which contains more than one pair in  
                         A 
                      , and the number of its pairs equals 2, 3, 4 or 5 (moreover, in the first case the two pairs are not opposite). Besides the  
                         A 
                      [ C2 ]-forced pairs,  
                         B 
                       must contain at least one other pair in order to make  Conditions C4 and C5  valid. For any given value of  
                         n 
                      , it is in principle possible to list all such possible pairs  
                         A 
                       and  
                         B 
                       (say up to relabeling of elements). However, even for small values of  
                         n 
                      , the task becomes quite tedious and from  
                         n 
                         = 
                         6 
                       the number of examples is daunting.

The strict weak order polytope  
                         
                            
                               
                                  P 
                               
                            
                            
                               
                                  SWO 
                               
                            
                            
                               n 
                            
                         
                       ( Regenwetter & Davis-Stober, 2012b ) is closely linked to still another polytope, the  weak order polytope  
                      
                         
                            
                               
                                  P 
                               
                            
                            
                               
                                  WO 
                               
                            
                            
                               n 
                            
                         
                       (studied in   Doignon & Fiorini, 2002; Fiorini & Fishburn, 2004; Regenwetter & Davis-Stober, 2008 ). A  weak order  (or  complete preorder ) on  
                         
                            [ 
                            n 
                            ] 
                         
                       is a relation which is reflexive, transitive and total. Thus the asymmetric part of a weak order is a strict weak order. The  weak order polytope  is defined (again in  
                         
                            
                               R 
                            
                            
                               
                                  
                                     A 
                                  
                                  
                                     n 
                                  
                               
                            
                         
                      ) by  
                         
                            
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        P 
                                     
                                  
                                  
                                     
                                        WO 
                                     
                                  
                                  
                                     n 
                                  
                               
                               = 
                               conv 
                               
                                  ( 
                                  
                                     { 
                                     
                                        
                                           χ 
                                        
                                        
                                           W 
                                        
                                     
                                     ∣ 
                                     W 
                                       is a weak order on   
                                     
                                        [ 
                                        n 
                                        ] 
                                     
                                     } 
                                  
                                  ) 
                               
                               . 
                            
                         
                       In fact, the polytopes  
                         
                            
                               
                                  P

                               n 
                            
                         
                       and

                               n

                                  2

                            … 
                            , 
                            
                               
                                  1 
                               
                               
                                  2 
                               
                            
                            ) 
                         
                       (because the complement of a strict weak order is a weak order, and reciprocally). Thus the inequality

In several primary FDI’s the independent term vanishes (in contrast to  Theorem 4 .1). Moreover, Example   
                         
                            
                               F

                       in  Fig. 14  invalidates  Conditions C1 and C3  from Section   4 , while Example

                         A 
                       does not always form a PC-graph—which complicates a lot the search for a classification of the primary FDIs of
